column8: Implement Plugin trait in Rust; consider subprocess JSON-RPC or WASM (wasmtime). Start with limited hooks: chat.message, tool.execute.before, permission.ask, shell.env. Provide plugin manifest and installer. Sandbox and permission model required.

column7: Add LSP client manager per project root. Maintain registry of servers->extensions. Use existing Rust LSP crates or spawn server processes communicating via stdio. Debounce diagnostics and surface via session API. CLI debug commands for diagnostics.

priority: Expose PluginApi for TUI with register_command, register_route, dialog primitives, kv store. Load plugins via dynamic IPC; for native Rust plugins provide plugin SDK. Ensure keybinds and theming hooks.

benefit: Read-only planning vs full-access execution Low-Medium
priority: Implement agent profiles with enforced permission policies. Plan agent denies file edits by default and prompts before shell commands. Enforce in tool/command execution paths.

benefit: Middleware to tune LLM/tool params per session Medium
priority: Add middleware chain or hook list invoked before LLM requests/tool executions. Allow plugins to modify temperature, headers, top_p, etc.

benefit: Enable switching providers and enterprise auth Medium
priority: Abstract Provider trait and Auth manager. Support OAuth and API key flows via pluggable auth handlers. Provide UI for provider selection and token management.
Implement plugin store dirs (global/project), CLI commands for add/remove/list/enable/disable. Track metadata fingerprint and last_time. For MVP support local dirs or git URLs.
Expose hook before compaction to add context strings or replace compaction prompt. Helpful for privacy redaction and focused summaries.

benefit: Tooling for LSP diagnostics and symbol search Low
priority: Add debug commands to print diagnostics, document symbols, workspace symbol search. Reuse LSP client manager.

benefit: Allow remote control of headless server High
priority: Design server API + auth tokens, allow multiple clients. Non-trivial: network, auth, session sync. Consider later-phase implementation.
